entry.title: Enhancing SAP GL Account Master Data: Adding Custom Fields Tab in FS00 Transaction
entry.content:
Edit SAP GL Account Centrally (T-code FS00) - Add Custom Fields in a New 'Additional Fields' Tab

In our enhancement, we extended the GL master data screen by adding a custom field, “Custom Long Text,” to overcome the 40-character limit of the standard text field. This new field is displayed in a new tab labeled "Additional Fields," which is available across all related transactions (FS00, FS01, FS02, and FS03) since they share the same layout.

We also developed an upload program for mass changes to the custom long text field: ZFI_GL_TEXT_UPLOAD. This program updates the custom field efficiently.

Key Points:

  • No screen exit or BADI is available to add new fields or tabs in the GL master screen.
  • However, the enhancement can be achieved through layout customization, which allows adding new tabs and fields.

Steps:

1. Add the Custom Field to Table and Structure

  • Append the custom field “ZDESC” (Custom Long Text) to table SKB1.
  • Append the same field to structure GLACCOUNT_CCODE_DATA for data transfer between the screen and the database.

 

2. Create a New Layout in SPRO

  • Navigate to SPRO:
    Financial Accounting → General Ledger Accounting → G/L Accounts → Master Data → Preparations → Additional Activities → Layouts for Individual Processing → Define Layout
  • Copy an existing layout and create a new layout, ZADD.

 

3. Update Layout Configuration in Tables

  • Update the following tables with the new layout details:
    • TAMLAY0: Add the new layout, setting application = “GL_MASTER” and layout = “ZADD.”
    • TAMLAY1: Define the new tab page (e.g., Tab 7 - "Additional Fields").
    • TAMLAY2: Set group box configuration for the custom screen (group box = ‘V0100’).

 

4. Create a Custom Function Group and Screen

  • Create a custom function group ZFI_FG in SE80.
  • Develop a new screen (e.g., 0100) for the custom field, “Custom Long Text.”
    • In the PBO module, use the structure GLACCOUNT_CCODE_DATA to transfer data from the database to the screen.
       

       

 

 

 

 

ASSIGN (‘(SAPLGL_ACCOUNT_MASTER_MAINTAIN) ac_new-CCODE_DATA-ZDESC’) TO <fs>. skb1-ZDESC = <fs>.

 

 

 

 

 
  • In the PAI module, transfer the data from the screen back to the structure.
     

 

 

 

 

<fs> = skb1-ZDESC.

 

 

 

Here is the full implementation code of the custom screen.

 

*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
***INCLUDE LZFI_FGO01.
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Module INITIATE_ADDITIONAL_FIELDS OUTPUT
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
MODULE initiate_additional_fields OUTPUT.
* SET PF-STATUS 'xxxxxxxx'.
* SET TITLEBAR 'xxx'.

*PBO

  ASSIGN ('(SAPLGL_ACCOUNT_MASTER_MAINTAIN)STATUS-ACTIVITY') TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_ctivity>).
  IF <fs_ctivity> IS ASSIGNED.
    LOOP AT SCREEN.
      CASE <fs_ctivity>.
        WHEN c_displaymode.
          screen-input = c_noinput.

        WHEN OTHERS.
          screen-input = c_input.
      ENDCASE.

      MODIFY SCREEN.
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDIF.

  ASSIGN ('(SAPLGL_ACCOUNT_MASTER_MAINTAIN)GLACCOUNT_SCREEN_KEY-SAKNR') TO <fs>.
  IF <fs> IS ASSIGNED. saknr = <fs>. ENDIF.
  ASSIGN ('(SAPLGL_ACCOUNT_MASTER_MAINTAIN)GLACCOUNT_SCREEN_KEY-KTOPL') TO <fs>.
  IF <fs> IS ASSIGNED. ktopl = <fs>. ENDIF.
  ASSIGN ('(SAPLGL_ACCOUNT_MASTER_MAINTAIN)GLACCOUNT_SCREEN_KEY-BUKRS') TO <fs>.
  IF <fs> IS ASSIGNED. bukrs = <fs>. ENDIF.

  "Read if saved previously
  IF it_skb1[] IS INITIAL.
    SELECT * FROM skb1 INTO TABLE it_skb1_orig WHERE bukrs = bukrs . "AND saknr = saknr.
    APPEND LINES OF it_skb1_orig TO it_skb1.
  ENDIF.

ENDMODULE.

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Module READ_ADDITIONAL_FIELDS OUTPUT
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
MODULE read_additional_fields OUTPUT.
  ASSIGN ('(SAPLGL_ACCOUNT_MASTER_MAINTAIN)GLACCOUNT_SCREEN_KEY-SAKNR') TO <fs>.
  IF <fs> IS ASSIGNED. saknr = <fs>. ENDIF.
  ASSIGN ('(SAPLGL_ACCOUNT_MASTER_MAINTAIN)GLACCOUNT_SCREEN_KEY-KTOPL') TO <fs>.
  IF <fs> IS ASSIGNED. ktopl = <fs>. ENDIF.
  ASSIGN ('(SAPLGL_ACCOUNT_MASTER_MAINTAIN)GLACCOUNT_SCREEN_KEY-BUKRS') TO <fs>.
  IF <fs> IS ASSIGNED. bukrs = <fs>. ENDIF.

  "fill skb1 from parent screen first

*  SELECT SINGLE * FROM skb1 INTO skb1 WHERE bukrs = bukrs AND saknr = saknr.

  READ TABLE it_skb1 INTO DATA(ls_skb1) WITH KEY saknr = saknr bukrs = bukrs .

  IF sy-subrc = 0.
*    zskb1-zdesc = skb1-zdesc.
    zskb1-zdesc = ls_skb1-zdesc.
  ELSE.
    CLEAR zskb1-zdesc.
  ENDIF.

  CLEAR ls_skb1.

*  ASSIGN ('(SAPLGL_ACCOUNT_MASTER_MAINTAIN)ac_new-CCODE_DATA-ZDESC') TO <fs>.
*from database to screen
*  skb1-zdesc = <fs>.


ENDMODULE.

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&      Module  UPDATE_ADDITIONAL_FIELDS  INPUT
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*       text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
MODULE update_additional_fields INPUT.
  ASSIGN ('(SAPLGL_ACCOUNT_MASTER_MAINTAIN)GLACCOUNT_SCREEN_KEY-SAKNR') TO <fs>.
  IF <fs> IS ASSIGNED. saknr = <fs>. ENDIF.
  ASSIGN ('(SAPLGL_ACCOUNT_MASTER_MAINTAIN)GLACCOUNT_SCREEN_KEY-KTOPL') TO <fs>.
  IF <fs> IS ASSIGNED. ktopl = <fs>. ENDIF.
  ASSIGN ('(SAPLGL_ACCOUNT_MASTER_MAINTAIN)GLACCOUNT_SCREEN_KEY-BUKRS') TO <fs>.
  IF <fs> IS ASSIGNED. bukrs = <fs>. ENDIF.
  "update zdesc from input field


**  break azaki.
*  CLEAR ls_skb1.
*  READ TABLE it_skb1 INTO ls_skb1 WITH KEY saknr = saknr bukrs = bukrs .
*
*  "MOVE-CORRESPONDING skb1 to ls_skb1.
*  ls_skb1-zdesc = zskb1-zdesc.
**  ls_skb1-saknr = saknr.
**  "ls_skb1-KTOPL = KTOPL.
**  ls_skb1-bukrs = bukrs.
*
*  MODIFY TABLE it_skb1 FROM ls_skb1." TRANSPORTING zdesc.
*  "skb1-zdesc = zskb1-zdesc.

  CLEAR ls_skb1.

  LOOP AT it_skb1 INTO ls_skb1 WHERE saknr = saknr AND bukrs = bukrs.
    ls_skb1-zdesc = zskb1-zdesc.
    MODIFY it_skb1 FROM ls_skb1 TRANSPORTING zdesc.
    CLEAR ls_skb1.
  ENDLOOP.
  CLEAR ls_skb1.

*"to update field in standard table
*  skb1-zdesc = zskb1-zdesc.
*  MODIFY table skb1 from zskb1 TRANSPORTING zdesc.

*  ASSIGN ('(SAPLGL_ACCOUNT_MASTER_MAINTAIN)SKB1-ZDESC') TO <fs>.
*  IF <fs> IS ASSIGNED. <fs> = zskb1-zdesc . ENDIF.
ENDMODULE.
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&      Module  USER_COMMAND_0100  INPUT
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*       text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
MODULE user_command_0100 INPUT.

  CASE sy-ucomm.

    WHEN 'SAVE'. "standard called this user_command_0100 also

      ASSIGN ('(SAPLGL_ACCOUNT_MASTER_MAINTAIN)GLACCOUNT_SCREEN_KEY-SAKNR') TO <fs>.
      IF <fs> IS ASSIGNED. saknr = <fs>. ENDIF.
      ASSIGN ('(SAPLGL_ACCOUNT_MASTER_MAINTAIN)GLACCOUNT_SCREEN_KEY-KTOPL') TO <fs>.
      IF <fs> IS ASSIGNED. ktopl = <fs>. ENDIF.
      ASSIGN ('(SAPLGL_ACCOUNT_MASTER_MAINTAIN)GLACCOUNT_SCREEN_KEY-BUKRS') TO <fs>.
      IF <fs> IS ASSIGNED. bukrs = <fs>. ENDIF.

      CLEAR ls_skb1.
      READ TABLE it_skb1 INTO ls_skb1 WITH KEY bukrs = bukrs saknr = saknr.
      IF sy-subrc = 0.
        MODIFY skb1 FROM ls_skb1 .
        COMMIT WORK.
      ENDIF.
  ENDCASE.
ENDMODULE.

 

5. Register the Custom Function Group

  • Add a new entry to table TAMLAYA, linking the new function group (ZFI_FG) to the GL master application.

 

6. Assign the Layout to a Chart of Accounts

  • In SPRO, assign the newly created layout to a specific chart of accounts: Financial Accounting → General Ledger Accounting → G/L Accounts → Master Data → Preparations → Additional Activities → Assign Layout to Chart of Accounts.

 

Final Result

After completing these steps, the GL master data screen (T-code FS00) will display a new tab labeled "Additional Fields" with the custom field “Custom Long Text.” This enhancement allows the input of long text exceeding the standard character limit.

Congratulations! You've successfully extended the G/L account to store descriptions up to 100 characters.

In the future, we'll explore the upload program for bulk updating the custom long text description of G/L accounts, allowing you to maintain multiple accounts efficiently at once.

I’ll also share how I enabled displaying and saving this custom field in the FIORI RAP Application "Manage G/L Account Master Data" through a FIORI Extension App.

Best Regards,
Abdelrahman Zaki

entry.title: TM S4HANA cost distribution for own trucks
entry.content:
Hello,

our client (shipper) uses subcontracting Freight order and Settlement with Cost distribution. He also has his own fleet of vehicles for which he would like to track transportation costs. Is it possible to create cost distribution document for own vehicles ( without purchase order+SES ) ?

Best regards,

Tomo

entry.title: Re: Restrict logon through RFC remote logon
entry.content: SAP has a whitepaper on how to secure RFC - please refer to Securing Remote Function Call (RFC) (sap.com)

entry.title: Re: SCM - S/4 Hana Private Cloud Implementation - Course/...
entry.content:
Hi @BhargavTadituri 
I reached out to our experts - I have copied their replied and pasted it to your questions 

  1. The supply chain-specific course ( Production Planning and Manufacturing ) comes with a cost, whereas the learner journey for Private Cloud implementation is free with an SAP S-User ID. Is my approach correct, or am I missing something?
    You can access free, self-paced learning journeys on the SAP Learning site to build and deepen your skills. Beyond this, you can sharpen your competitive edge with expert guidance and gain access to practice systems, live sessions, and premium learning resources through a subscription to SAP Learning Hub with included certification attempts and stay certified materials to maximize your earning potential with future-proof SAP solution skills. To further expand your expertise, you can take advantage of scheduled live instructor-led courses guided by experienced trainers.
  2. I have also begun the prerequisite courses for the certification. Are the contents provided in the learner journey sufficient for exam preparation?
    Yes, because assessment and certification items must be created from the content of the previous learning journey.
  3. Learning Journey on how to prepare for SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP Business Network for Supply Chain Shows only 4hr worth of content is it really sufficient for the assessment preparation.
    Yes, because assessment and certification items must be created from the content of the previous learning journey.
  4. Do any of these certificate act as prerequisite before pursuing specific SCM certificates. Taking into account that some few learnings are free and few have a cost associated either by paying for learning Hub or E-learning.
    No for our Supply Chain certifications, no other certifications are prerequisites to it: Browse SAP Certifications

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: How to setup S/4HANA Cloud to get market rates (e.g. exchange rates) automatically
entry.content:
I just went through this whole process yesterday without having seen this blog. If I only had found this a little sooner, my life would have been a lot easier!

Good job on getting everything together! The only thing you didn't mention that I stumbled over is the fact that the BYOR BTP service is currently only offered in the AWS - Europe (Frankfurt) region. So make sure to create a subaccount in that region if you want this service. For us in the Americas this is usually not the first choice...

Keep up the good work!

entry.title: Re: How to upgrade SAP diagnostic agent.
entry.content: With option 1 you'll keep the old exe directory with kernel and sapjvm. So you have to change them manually.

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud - Deactivating users programmatically
entry.content:
Thanks to new features delivered with the Quarterly Release Update (QRC) in Q3 2024 the SCIM API now supports deactivating users.

This was a popular request and received a good number of votes in the Influence Portal (idea number 304030).

The ‘isActive’ user property was previously read-only, but now we can change this setting on a user basis via the user's endpoint for both version 1 and version 2 of the SCIM API.

 

Updated Sample Scripts

I have added 4 new sample scripts to my article ‘SAP Analytics Cloud User and Team Provisioning SCIM API Best Practices and Sample Scripts’ that enable you to automate the deactivation of users and teams of users.

The 4 new samples added are:

  • SCIM 1402-U-U-Ua-Fcj-Es-Update User Active status
  • SCIM 2402-U-U-Ua-Fcj-Es-Update User Active status
  • SCIM 1452-TU-U-Ua-Fcj-Es-Update Team Active status
  • SCIM 2452-TU-U-Ua-Fcj-Es-Update Team Active status

The samples 1xxx, use version 1 of the SCIM API, and the samples 2xxx, use version 2.

Samples x402 update a user's active status one at a time, while samples x452 update a whole team of users.

For those budding API enthusiasts, the version 2 samples use the PATCH and BULK operations.

 

Updated Scenarios

Whilst the samples above are helpful, the real value is my updated ‘scenarios’ that help manage users' life cycles, particularly the deactivation and eventual deletion of dormant users. A scenario is a straightforward concept comprising sets of pre-configured sample data files. Each scenario addresses a single use case by combining different sample scripts (Postman collections) in a particular order.

It means most of the thinking has been done for you. All you need to do is tweak the data files for your needs.

There are 5 updated scenarios for managing dormant users, as this topic is surprisingly complex. At a high level, this diagram shows what is possible.

 

The image above shows when users were created and their historic login activity. This table explains the logic determining if the user is dormant or not:

User 1

User-created over 3 months ago,without any logins since creation.The user is deactivated.

User 2

User-created under 3 months,without any logins since creation.No changes made

User 3

User-created over 3 months ago andhas 2 or fewer logins within the last 90 days.The user has no login within the last 30 days.The user is deactivated.

User 4

User-created over 3 months ago, buthas 3 logins within the last 90 days anda login within the last 30 days.No changes made

User 5

User-created over 3 months ago andhas 2 or fewer logins within the last 90 days, butthere was a login within the last 30 days.No changes made

User 6

User-created over 3 months ago, buthas 4 logins within the last 90 days, althoughno login within the last 30 days.No changes made

User 7

User-created over 6 months ago,without any logins since creation and is already deactivated.The user is deleted

User 8

User-created under 6 months,without any logins since creation and is already deactivated.No changes made

User 9

User-created over 6 months ago andhas 2 or fewer logins within the last 120 days.The user has no login within the last 60 days and is already deactivated.The user is deleted
User 10User-created over 6 months ago, buthas 3 logins within the last 120 days anda login within the last 60 days.The user is deactivated.
User 11User-created over 6 months ago andhas 2 or fewer logins within the last 120 days, butthere was a login within the last 60 days.The user is deactivated.
User 12User-created over 6 months ago,has 2 logins within the last 120 days, althoughno login within the last 60 days. The user is already deactivated.No changes made

 Please visit this updated article SAP Analytics Cloud – Managing dormant users for the next level of detail.

Alternatively, if you can't wait to get started, download the user guide which provides step-by-step instructions from this blog post (search for 'Sample Scripts User Guide').

entry.title: Partner Delivery Manager (PDM) Newsletter GROW with SAP - October 2024 Edition
entry.content:
Welcome to the PDM Newsletter GROW with SAP Blog

We're excited to introduce our monthly newsletter blog, your go-to resource for staying connected with the latest innovations, insights, and trends in the GROW with SAP ecosystem. Each edition is a selection of exclusive expert guidance, inspiring stories, and actionable content designed to help you enhance your delivery management skills and develop your partner delivery practice to stay ahead in the ever-evolving world of SAP.

We encourage you to become an active member of our vibrant community by joining our partner groups, and in particular for cloud ERP our Implementation knowledge, experience sharing and  Q&A group: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/ct-p/s4hana-cloud-public 

Let's connect and grow together!

Kind regards / Cordialement / Mit freundlichen Grüßen,

Sebastian Sauer

Partner Delivery Manager GROW with SAP (SAP S/4HANA Cloud) - EMEA Region

SAP Ecosystem Delivery Success

 

Note:  Links require access to SAP PartnerEdge or SAP Learning Hub

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition    #GROW with SAP#                                                                                                                                                             

Top Highlights

Overview

       Register/Access

 

 

 

 

 

SAP Enterprise Cloud Services – Partner Summit 2024 (EMEA/MEE)

SAP Enterprise Cloud Services Partner Summit 2024' is a virtual summit to empower our SAP PartnerEdge, Sell and SAP PartnerEdge, Service partners. It will be scenario-based training comprised of some of the topics listed below.

            Register

 

  • Session: 15th October 2024
    09:00 AM to 12:00 PM CET

 

  • Session: 16th  October 2024
    09:00 AM to 11:30 PM CET

 

 

Highlights of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Release

The second major release of the year 2024 is here! The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release is filled with innovations that boost your productivity and support your everyday work life.

We are thrilled to give you further insights on our fully-fledged cloud ERP, supporting your journey to the intelligent, sustainable enterprise in this blog post.

 

          Access

 

Webinar: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Implementation Essentials for Finance

Welcome to the implementation essentials event by expertise services on the topic of finance. This session is to support project teams preparing for, or involved in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementations.

This is a 3-day event, each day is 2.5 hours, and will include the following topic areas related to the finance line of business:

  • Key finance implementation decisions will be discussed (ledger/parallel accounting/chart of accounts/fiscal year variant/currencies)
  • Update to date major innovations included (financial accounting/management accounting/financial operations/cash/group reporting/closing/AI etc.)
  • Presenters will share implementation experience and lessons learned

         Register

  • Session 1: 15th October 2024, 9:00 AM to 11:30 AM Europe/Amsterdam
  • Session 2: 16th October 2024, 9:00 AM to 11:30 AM Europe/Amsterdam
  • Session 3: 17th October 2024, 9:00 AM to 11:30 AM Europe/Amsterdam

 

Partner Learning Series: Optimizing SAP Portfolio in the Public Sector

The Partner Learning Series is tailored to elevate the learning experiences of our partners seeking to maximize the use of the SAP Portfolio in the Public Sector. Our on-demand sessions, each lasting approximately 20 minutes, provide a deeper understanding of transitioning government customers to the Cloud in collaboration with experienced industry experts. You'll have the flexibility to learn at your own pace about SAP's Strategy, priorities, and trends, including SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) and cloud deployment options.

The series delves into SAP S/4HANA Cloud, RISE with SAP, GROW with SAP, and SAP Business AI within the Public Sector industry, with a focus on grants and funds management, SAP SuccessFactors HCM, sourcing and procurement, and citizen services. Join us as we explore new insights and strategies to drive success in the Public Sector.

        Access

 

 

 

 

 

 

  
       

Events and Webinars for Partners

Overview

    Register/Access

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Webinar: GROW with SAP with Concur Expense – deep-dive session

Discover the exciting opportunity we now have to offer the value of Concur Expense to our GROW with SAP premium customers. During this session we will cover:

  • GROW with SAP w/Concur entitlement & Concur partner programs
  • Concur Expense product overview and SAP S/4HANA w/Expense value prop
  • Integration and implementation
  • The opportunity and next steps

 

     Replay

  • Session 1: 3rd October 2024, 09:00 AM - 10:00 AM Europe/Berlin
  • Session 2: 3rd October 2024, 5:00 PM - 6:00 PM Europe/Berlin

 

 

Webinar: Explore GROW with SAP entitlements - SAP Analytics Cloud for planning

SAP Analytics Cloud is the one planning solution combining business intelligence, augmented analytics, and built-in collaboration tools for everyone to work together and make faster, confident decisions. Learn more about them in this session

    Replay

  • Session 1: 4th October 2024,  12:00 AM - 13:00 AM SGT

 

 

Webinar: SAP Supply Chain Management for GROW with SAP

This session will provide you with an overview of GROW with SAP, and how SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition helps companies with the embedded Supply Chain Management capabilities. The focus of the session is to introduce the complementing solutions from the SAP Supply Chain Management portfolio. These solutions integrate with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and provide a perfect upsell opportunity for customers who have specific requirements in Supply Chain Management processes. You will learn about how to position these solutions in the GROW with SAP context, and about customer success stories.

     Replay

 

  • Session 1: 7th October 2024, 4:00 PM GMT - 5:00 PM Europe/Berlin

 

 

 

 

Webinar: Introduction to SAP Information Lifecycle Management (SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Hot Topic Series)

During this session our experts will provide insights on the "Why" and "How" of SAP Information Lifecycle Management. We will discuss possible use-cases and provide an overview of features and functions. Additionally, we will showcase the differences between SAP ILM in an SAP S/4HANA on-prem or private cloud deployment vs. an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition deployment.  For example, you will learn about the SAP ILM advisor which provides guidance for the data volume aspect of SAP ILM and get to know about archive file storage infrastructure. 

 

    Replay

  • Session 1: 8th October 2024,  10:00 AM - 11:00 AM Europe/Amsterdam

 

 

 

 

 

 

Webinar: Expand the value of GROW with SAP with add-on packages for chief financial officer, chief supply chain officer and chief human resources officer

In this session we will review currently offered GROW with SAP packages in financial management, digital supply chain and human resources. We will update you on the changes since the packages were launched earlier this year. You will also have an opportunity to ask questions.

     Replay

  • Session: 9th October 2024, 09:00 AM - 10:30 AM CET

 

 

 

Webinar: SAP Project and Resource Management for GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (deep dive)

After the training you will know how SAP Project and Resource Management integrates with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Professional Services scenario. How project staffing is done, which apps are provided and the key capabilities. Further you will learn which integration scenarios are available and how to proceed to setup your integration scenario..

       Replay

  • Session 1: 9th October 2024, 02:30 PM - 05:00 PM Asia/Singapore
 

 

 

Webinar: Explore GROW with SAP Entitlements – SAP Ariba Buying, SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management

GROW with SAP entitlements offer support for customers looking to quickly and predictably adopt cloud ERP with continuous innovation. SAP Ariba Buying, base edition creates a smart and simple purchasing experience that increases user engagement. SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management on SAP Business Technology Platform is seamlessly integrated to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and provides a single point of access and visibility to supplier invoices from multiple connected systems. Learn more about them in this session

 

      Replay

  • Session 1: 9th October 2024, 12:00 PM - 01:00 PM Asia/Singapore

 

 

Webinar: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Integration & Extensibility Fundamentals

In this interactive webinar, you will gain understanding on aspects & capabilities of Integration & Extensibility within SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. This webinar will start with an introduction to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud expertise services community, outlining the importance Integration & Extensibility in SAP Activate methodology, integration types and extensibility types – followed with an opportunity to interact and raise questions.

       Register

  • Session 1: 28th October 2024, 04:00 PM CET - 05:30 PM CET

 

 

Webinar: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Implementation Essentials for Sourcing & Procurement 

Welcome to the Implementation Essentials event by Expertise Services on the topic of Sourcing & Procurement. This session is to support Partner project teams preparing for or involved in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementations.

       Register

  • Session 1: 23rd October 2024, 3:00 PM - 5:00 PM Europe/Amsterdam
  • Session 2: 24th October 2024, 3:00 PM - 5:00 PM Europe/Amsterdam

 

 

Transformation with S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Overview

    Register/Access

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Find Release Information & Content with the Release Navigator for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 

Join us for an interactive virtual live session where we’ll walk you through the features and benefits of the Release Navigator. Discover how this tool provides a comprehensive view of upcoming releases, allowing you to easily plan and prepare for updates. You’ll also learn how to find release scheduling and maintenance windows within the release navigator, enabling you to better plan for and manage the deployment of updates.

 

       Access

 

 

Advanced Solution Bundling for Solution Order Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

 

Check out the latest blog post on our newsletter about the new features introduced in Solution Order Management with the rollout of the 2402 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. This update supports the transition towards solution-based business models, offering a broad scope and range of customizable offerings. Find out more about how these business models drive competitive advantage and how the new solution bundles functionality in the 2402.3 release enables more efficient selling and processing of holistic solutions. Don't miss out on this insightful read.

 

       Access

 

 

Transportation Management for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408

Don't miss out on the latest product updates for Transportation Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408! We are thrilled to introduce the integration of Returns Purchase Orders with Transportation Management, enabling you to seamlessly perform transportation planning, monitor execution, and settle freight costs for returns purchase orders as part of the Return to Supplier process (scope item BMK). Stay tuned for more details on these exciting features.

 

       Access

 

 

 

 

Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408

Discover the latest innovations in Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management with the release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408. Our blog post outlines key topics, including reserving material using project demand (4RF), creating a project by copying an existing project using an API (1NT, 1YF), and integration with Project Plan Collaboration (6JN). Stay updated and explore how these innovations can elevate your project management experience.

 

       Access

 

 

 

 

 

Artificial Intelligence & Technology in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 

Check out the latest blog post on Artificial Intelligence and Technologies, highlighting the newest features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Release 2408. Our release introduces a diverse range of AI-driven functionalities that are designed to elevate user experience, enhance operational efficiency, and foster better decision-making. With features such as Joule, AI-assisted financial insights, and intelligent resource matching, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Release 2408 promises to optimize your business operations, making them more intuitive, accurate, and efficient. We extend our utmost appreciation to our dedicated developers for their hard work and commitment

 

        Access

 

 

SAP Recommendation: Assignment of Corporate Accounting Principle to Ledger

Check out our latest blog post, which provides guidance on the SAP Recommendation for assigning Corporate Accounting Principles to the Ledgers for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition (Public Cloud). entry.title: How to move all stock to Quarantine or scrap if we have new revision
entry.content:
Here the business is using ECN and they do revision of materials. If they create a new revision with ECN for one material then the stock of the old revision is to be scrapped or moved to a quarantine storage location. Is there any easy way to do this in background in standard?

Example: I got an iPhone 15. Later I got a new iPhone 16. Now I would like to scrap or move the iphone 15 to quarantine to do rework and convert it to iPhone 16.

Also is there any ongoing production order going on for iPhone 15, I have to hold it and make the change then and there itself.

Any Idea?

entry.title: RAP Hierarchy - Building a Tree
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm trying to build a tree in RAP Abap. I am on S/4 HANA 2023 ON-PREMISE system and I was able to make the connection with the annotation in entity ZR_C_TREE_VRAGENLIJST_R (projection view): 

@odata.hierarchy.recursiveHierarchy: [{ entity.name: 'ZR_H_TREE_HIERARCHY' }]

However, by making my Service Binding, based on the Service Definition, I'm getting following error :

"Error during compilation.  See next line for details.  Error in entity 'ZR_C_TREE_VRAGENLIJST_R(CDS): Hierarchy for view ZR_C_TREE_VRAGENLIJST_R is not supported"  

Is there something missing, or what can I do to make this work?

greets,

Helga

entry.title: Come Say Hi at an SAP TechEd Tour Stop
entry.content:
It’s a wrap! Yes, sadly, SAP TechEd Virtual is already over, but it’s been a blast! But the good news is: If you want to continue to learn, network, and bring out your best, you can still participate in one of five SAP TechEd on Tour or SAPinsider events worldwide – with learning sessions and meet-ups with SAP experts. Here’s what you need to know to come and say “Hi”: 

SAP is offering five separate tour stops around the globe, organized in collaboration with SAP user groups. Each stop will offer hands-on learning opportunities, deep-dive lecture sessions, and meet-the-experts all presented by SAP experts, partners, and many customers. We are also bringing back the Developer Garage for you as a great opportunity to learn by doing. 

Choose the US or Europe to meet SAP experts 

From November 12-14, join technical practitioners and SAP experts at ASUG Tech Connect 2024 in West Palm Beach for immersive sessions, SAP insights, and networking. Over three days, you'll enhance your skills, stay at the forefront of digital advancements, and grow professionally. 

If you prefer a trip to Europe, SAPinsider EMEA 2024 in Copenhagen, Denmark, is for you. From November 12-14, you can develop the skills and expertise needed to thrive in your role. Hear from customers about their successful digital transformations, learn implementation best practices from SAP partners, and connect with peers and SAP experts in a three-day learning event aligned with SAP TechEd Virtual.  

Get an overview of the SAP ecosystem in Australia 

If you fancy a trip to Down Under, we’re excited to meet you at SAUG-x 2024 this year! From November 19-20 you can learn from unique insights, network with your peers, hear from SAP experts, and get an overview of our ecosystem. Don’t miss these relevant knowledge sharing, learning, and networking sessions – all in Melbourne, Australia! 

And if you’re still in the mood for SAP TechEd by December, join us in Birmingham, UK, at UKISUG Connect 2024. At the ICC in Birmingham, expect to learn from experts, network with like-minded peers and collaborate together. Benefit from knowledge-rich keynotes, engaging breakout sessions, a range of workshops and an exhibition hall of over 70 SAP partners. 

Last, but not least, head over to the motherland of SAP – Germany – and join us at DSAG-TechXchange 2025 on April 4, 2025! Yes, it might be a couple of months away, but having your next trip planned is always a nice thing, don’t you think? 

Whichever location you prefer – we are looking forward to seeing you there! Make use of the unique opportunity to connect with like-minded individuals, gain valuable insights into the tech industry, and get hands-on experience with the latest SAP technologies. Whether you're a developer, architect, IT manager, or business executive, SAP TechEd on Tour has something for everyone. Don't miss out on this opportunity to stay ahead of the curve and propel your tech career to new heights! 

For more information on the SAP TechEd on Tour stops, visit this page. 

And while you’re at it, make sure to attend SAP CodeJam events in your area - free, hands-on coding experiences where you can collaborate with fellow developers to solve real-world challenges while diving deep into the latest SAP technologies.

entry.title: SAP BTP ABAP Environment – Pre-Upgrade Option for Release 2411
entry.content:
Overview

Providing a significant number of new features is an important goal of release upgrades to deliver innovations to our customers and partners. There are four major release upgrades per year following a predefined schedule as described in this blog post.

An upgrade is intended as a “no event” for customers and partners. For example, only compatible changes of released objects (APIs) will be delivered. We recommend performing a test of the custom-built productive apps in a test environment for the new release. The complexity of the technical dependencies between the custom-built apps and the underlying environment cannot be tested isolated, only including the ABAP environment on SAP-side. Therefore, we offer you to pre-upgrade selected systems 15 days* in advance to test the applications prior to the planned standard-upgrade.

* As an exceptional case for 2411, the pre-upgrade happens on Thursday instead of Friday (like in the recent upgrades). This leads to 16 days in advance prior to the planned standard-upgrade.   

 

Purpose

This option enables a customer or partner to test the applications they have already developed on top of the new release a couple of days before the standard-upgrade. With SAP BTP ABAP Environment (aka "Steampunk"), we follow the approach of keeping released objects stable and provide all enhancements in a compatible fashion. Early testing is an additional measure to ensure proper software quality and might be used to detect unforeseen regressions. However, this option is not intended to test new features or to consume new features earlier.

 

Approach

The standard-upgrade of all systems to release 2411 is planned for November 16/17.

A customer or partner can nominate one or more already existing systems for the pre-upgrade. A nominated system will be upgraded on October 31 within a planned downtime from 8:00 to 16:00 CET.

We recommend nominating a non-development system, such as a test system. The nomination of a development system is technically also possible, but fixes or other changes must not be imported into another system with a lower release. The nomination of a productive system doesn’t make sense because the purpose of the pre-upgrade is the re-test of the existing application before the productive system gets the new release.

According to the approach explained above, this offer is only relevant for customers or partners having at least two systems.

There might be situations where a correction of custom objects needs to be deployed. Such corrections are possible if the development / correction system is on the older release and is imported into the pre-upgraded system with the higher release.

Except potential fixes, the pre-upgrade is based on the same version as the standard-upgrade.

 

Costs

This offering is optional and free of any additional charges.

 

Planned New Features

The planned new features will be published on the What's New for SAP BTP ABAP Environment on October  18. This list gives you an overview of potential areas for re-testing. The purpose of the pre-upgrade is not to consume these features before the standard-upgrade, because the documentation on help.sap.com will be delivered later before the standard-upgrade. It would also not be possible to transport code of such an adoption, because subsequent systems are not yet on the new release during the pre-upgrade phase.

Independent of the upgrades of the SAP BTP ABAP Environment, we recommend developing automated tests for the custom-built apps to detect regressions early on and without manual effort for re-testing. Such automated tests are perfectly suited to be run in the pre-upgrade test phase.

 

Time Schedule

The following figure summarizes the general approach:

 

Milestones for release 2411:

  • October 4: Nomination of system(s) is possible in the Landscape Portal
  • October 18: Announcement of the planned new features
  • October 28: Pre-upgrade systems are known
  • October 31: Pre-upgrade
  • November 13: Potential decision to postpone the upgrade
  • November 16/17: Standard-upgrade

 

System Nomination

To nominate a system, please register the system(s) by October 28 (EOB) latest by using the Landscape Portal. You can find further information about Accessing the Landscape Portal and about Register Systems for Pre-Upgrade. Please do not create customer tickets for system nomination like in previous pre-upgrades.

With such a nomination, the customer agrees to an extraordinary planned maintenance on October 31 (see above).

 

Report an Issue

Issues should be reported as part of the regression test phase by November 8 (EOB) to allow for a sufficient time frame for issue analysis and potential corrections on SAP-side.

Please create a customer ticket by using component BC-CP-ABA.

If a detected issue is likely to endanger the productive use after the standard-upgrade, you should use ticket priority very high despite the fact that the pre-upgrade system is not a productive system.

 

Exceptional Process in Case of Unresolved Priority Very High Issues

In case of the aligned decision on Wednesday, November 13 (based on unresolved priority very high tickets) to postpone the upgrade, a dedicated mitigation process needs to be started together with the respective customer or partner, with the following boundary conditions:

  • Resolving the open priority very high issues remains the highest priority for both, SAP and the customer or partner, as it is also the prerequisite for the upgrade.
  • According to the estimated issue resolution, catching up the upgrade needs to be planned.
  • The upgrade leads to a business downtime of 6 – 8 hours.
  • The upgrade must be scheduled within 7 calendar days the latest after fixing all remaining open priority very high issues.
  • We will not provide any fixes for the old release after the original date of the standard-upgrade to the new release.

In case business-critical issues are detected after the upgrade:

  • Issues need to be reported as priority very high issues and will be processed accordingly.
  • Going back to the release before upgrade is not an option. Therefore, resolving the issues in the new release with the appropriate priority is the required approach.

 

Summary

We recommend performing regression tests in pre-upgraded test systems to avoid day-1 impacts of productive custom apps after the standard-upgrade.

entry.title: Re: get_ddic_reference( ) returns blanks
entry.content:
No issue for me.

Minimum reproducible example:

REPORT ztest.
DATA table_sflight TYPE TABLE OF sflight.

SELECT * FROM sflight INTO TABLE @table_sflight.
CALL METHOD cl_salv_table=>factory
  IMPORTING
    r_salv_table = DATA(salv)
  CHANGING
    t_table      = table_sflight.
DATA(ddic_reference) = salv->get_columns( )->get_column( 'CARRID' )->get_ddic_reference( ).
ASSERT ddic_reference = VALUE salv_s_ddic_reference( field = 'CARRID'
                                                     table = 'SFLIGHT' ).

entry.title: Re: get_ddic_reference( ) returns blanks
entry.content:
You forgot some code, try some code such as

 

" defintions
DATA: cols TYPE REF TO cl_salv_columns_table,
      col1 TYPE REF TO cl_salv_column,
      ddic TYPE salv_s_ddic_reference.
" call methods (you can merge in one statement)
cols = alv->get_columns( ).
col1 = cols->get_column( 'MY_COLUMN' ).
ddic = col1->get_ddic_reference( ).

 

Of course field MY_COLUMN must exist in the internal table and use some ddic reference in its declaration.

entry.title: How to create a Catchup Bill in SAP Subscription Billing
entry.content:
Hi

Im working with prospect usecase where I need to show how SB will be able to process a 'catchup bill'

SCENARIO Explained

  • Consider a sample customer with a monthly music subscription charge of £30, to be billed monthly in advance.
  • For customers that go live before the 15th of a month, prospect charges for the full month in which they go live. For customers that go live after the 15th, charges are not incurred until the 1st of the following month.
    • In this case, assume the customer's installation went live on 12th September, therefore they should be charged £30 for the month of September.
  • Prospect currently generates its recurring invoices 1 month and 1 day in advance of the billing period. For this customer, the first recurring invoice run would therefore be triggered on 30th September covering the period from 1st to 30th November. This means that:
    • a "catch-up" invoice for £60 is required at the end of September to bill for the two months from 1st September to 31st October, in addition to the recurring invoice amount of £30 for the month of November;
    • the £30 in recurring revenue for November needs to be deferred, and recognised in November.
  • Currently, prospect produces two separate invoice documents (one for the catch-up period and one for November, in this example). In future, a single invoice could cover the whole period, provided the ERP system can recognise the revenue in the right periods (i.e. catch-up amount recognised in the current month and advance payments recognised in the relevant time period). SAP Subscription Billing

entry.title: Re: Work in Progress (WIP) account - G/L account type
entry.content:
Hello @zhenghaoMa, WIP posting are just for adjustment to ensure we show correct profits (To credit production cost posted in P/L for time being no final GR done & debit offsetting balance sheet G/L) which are reversed once production finished.

If WIP P/L GL's are created as cost element cost objects assignment needed but no allocation/distribution needed for such posting as ultimately balance of these GL's are zero.

Regards,

Sumesh Gupta

entry.title: Um Evento que Encheu os Olhos da Comunidade SAP em Recife #SITREC2024
entry.content:
No último dia 5 de outubro, Recife recebeu de braços abertos mais uma edição do SAP Inside Track, que não acontecia desde 2020. Esse evento, que já é uma tradição para a comunidade SAP no Brasil desde 2009, chegou à sua 43ª edição cercado de grande expectativa. E o público, formado por profissionais da área em Recife, respondeu à altura: os 100 ingressos destinados ao público geral esgotaram-se menos de um mês antes do evento.

O SIT Recife 2024 aconteceu nas dependências da Uninassau, que nos acolheu com muita atenção e cordialidade, tivemos o patrocínio da MakeValue, GFX, Tachyonix e Accenture. E também com o apoio da Moovi Education. Cerca de 150 participantes estiveram presentes, e o sucesso foi tanto que já começamos a planejar o próximo encontro. A empolgação da comunidade nos faz refletir sobre os pontos a melhorar para as próximas edições, mas também nos motiva a continuar promovendo essa festa da comunidade SAP na cidade de Recife.

Logo na abertura, fomos brindados com uma apresentação de uma orquestra de frevo, a tradicional dança de Recife, conhecida nacional e internacionalmente. Ao som de frevos clássicos, dois passistas animaram o público com suas acrobacias enquanto os participantes desfrutavam de um café da manhã caprichado. A festa ficou ainda mais tecnológica com a presença de um robô da Accenture, um dos patrocinadores do evento, que também arriscou alguns passos de frevo!

Além das palestras, o evento foi um momento especial para reencontros. Profissionais da comunidade SAP, muitos dos quais só conhecíamos virtualmente, tiveram a chance de se encontrar pessoalmente, trocar experiências e tirar muitas fotos para registrar esses momentos. Foi incrível ver o networking acontecendo de forma tão natural.

O evento contou com a presença de @marco_v_santos , que abriu o evento falando sobre as inovações na plataforma da comunidade SAP, destacando como essas mudanças facilitam a interação entre profissionais em suas respectivas localidades. Ele também aproveitou para divulgar o grupo da comunidade SAP de Recife, criado para que todos possam compartilhar blogs, discussões e novidades sobre o mundo SAP.

Os organizadores desta edição @Marssel700 , ao lado de @RobertaFernande , Helder Lins e @afigueredo54, e também o palestrante João Otto demos boas-vindas aos participantes. Além disso, tivemos a Cláudia Danielle como mestre de cerimônia, conduzindo as atividades de palco com muita habilidade e simpatia.

As palestras mantiveram o público atento até o fim. Em eventos desse porte, é comum que o número de participantes diminua ao longo do dia, já que muitos escolhem assistir apenas a palestras específicas. No entanto, nesta edição, o interesse foi tanto que a maioria do público permaneceu até o último minuto, e na foto final, o auditório estava cheio! Isso demonstra o quanto a comunidade estava engajada e ávida por conhecimento.

A agenda do evento foi diversificada, cobrindo temas de grande relevância para o mercado SAP. Confira abaixo a lista das palestras que tivemos:

 

Palestrante - Link da Apresentação gravada no Youtube:

✔️ Key Note - Credenciamento e abertura
✔️ Marssel Vilaça - Maximizando o Valor do S/4HANA
✔️ Jonys Arcanjo - Inteligência Artificial e SAP Data Intelligence: Revolucionando a Gestão de Crédito
✔️ Lucas Guedes Ferreira - Clean Core, SAP BTP e como a Tachyonix é a peça que faltava nesse quebra-cabeça
✔️Manuel Robalinho - Business innovation challenges and impacts for S/4HANA
✔️Antonio Figueredo e João Paulo Cabral - Facilita com BTP no Grupo Cornélio Brennand
✔️Frederico Gurgel - GFX Pagamento com cartões integrado ao S/4HANA Public Edition
✔️Danilo C. Bispo - Desmitificando o SAP DRC NFe - Como se preparar para uma migração?
✔️João Otto Gethmann Jr e Alan Dias - Inovação em SAP

Ainda temos os Podcasts com palestrantes do SITREC2024

🎙️Episódio 01 - Live Track Podcast: Clean Core com SAP BTP para otimizar processos empresariais

🎙️Episódio 02 - Live Track: Inteligência Artificial e SAP Data Intelligence para Gestão de Crédito

Como é tradição, o evento foi encerrado com sorteios de brindes. Entre os itens sorteados estavam livros da SAP Press, cursos da Moovi Education e equipamentos eletrônicos da MakeValue. Foi um final empolgante para um dia que certamente ficará na memória de todos os presentes.

Essa foi a quarta edição do SAP Inside Track em Recife, e o evento só confirma o quanto o mercado SAP cresce na região, com a chegada de novos parceiros e a capacitação de grandes profissionais para implementar as tecnologias mais recentes. Mal podemos esperar pela próxima edição, que já está nos planos para 2024.

Todas as Fotos (All Photos)

Enquanto isso, fique de olho nos eventos Warm up SITFOR, SIT Fortaleza e SIT Curitiba, que estão a todo vapor! Ainda dá tempo de participar dessa grande confraternização da comunidade SAP, cheia de aprendizado e networking. Não deixe de seguir os links abaixo para ficar por dentro de tudo o que acontece no mundo SAP aqui no Brasil.

Páginas Linkedin:

https://www.linkedin.com/in/sitbrazil/

https://www.youtube.com/@SAPInsideTrack

https://www.linkedin.com/groups/12467481/

https://www.linkedin.com/events/sapinsidetrackcuritiba20247244806570096640002/

Canais do Youtube

https://www.youtube.com/@SAPInsideTrack

https://www.youtube.com/@sitfor-sapinsidetrackforta6071

 

Agradecemos a todos que puderam participar e aqueles que não puderam, não percam os próximos eventos. Sigam o nosso grupo para ficarem por dentro de todas as novidades. Abraço a todos!

entry.title: Re: Um Evento que Encheu os Olhos da Comunidade SAP em Recife #SITREC2024
entry.content:
On October 5th, Recife warmly welcomed another edition of the SAP Inside Track, marking its return since 2020. This event, a staple for the SAP community in Brazil since 2009, reached its 43rd edition amid great anticipation. The excitement was palpable, as the 100 tickets available to the public sold out in less than a month!

All Pictures

The SIT Recife 2024 took place at Uninassau, which graciously hosted us with great hospitality. We were proudly sponsored by MakeValue, GFX, Tachyonix, and Accenture, with additional support from Moovi Education. Approximately 150 participants attended, and the event was such a success that we are already planning the next gathering! The community’s enthusiasm inspires us to reflect on areas for improvement while motivating us to continue fostering this celebration of SAP in Recife.

We kicked off the day with a lively performance by a traditional frevo orchestra, showcasing Recife’s renowned dance, celebrated both nationally and internationally. As attendees enjoyed a delicious breakfast, two dancers captivated the audience with their acrobatics, adding to the festive atmosphere. The event also featured a robot from Accenture, one of our sponsors, who joined in the frevo fun!

In addition to the engaging presentations, the event served as a special reunion. Many professionals from the SAP community, most of whom we only knew virtually, had the chance to meet face-to-face, exchange experiences, and capture plenty of photos to commemorate these moments. It was incredible to witness networking happen so naturally.

The event featured @marco_v_santos, who opened with insights on innovations within the SAP community platform, emphasizing how these changes enhance interaction among professionals in their localities. He also promoted the Recife SAP community group, created for sharing blogs, discussions, and updates in the SAP realm.

Our organizing team for this edition @Marssel700 @RobertaFernande  Helder Lins  and @afigueredo54  along with speaker João Otto, welcomed the participants. We were also fortunate to have Cláudia Danielle as our master of ceremonies, skillfully leading the stage activities with warmth and char

The presentations kept the audience engaged until the very end. At large events like this, it’s common for attendance to dwindle as the day goes on, with many opting to attend only specific talks. However, this edition saw such high interest that most of the audience remained until the final moment, filling the auditorium for our concluding photo! This turnout illustrates the community's commitment and eagerness for knowledge.

The event agenda covered highly relevant topics in the SAP market. Here’s a glimpse of the presentations:

Keynote - Registration and Opening
Marssel Vilaça - Maximizing the Value of S/4HANA
Jonys Arcanjo - AI and SAP Data Intelligence: Revolutionizing Credit Management
Lucas Guedes Ferreira - Clean Core, SAP BTP, and how Tachyonix is the missing piece of the puzzle
Manuel Robalinho - Business Innovation Challenges and Impacts for S/4HANA
Antonio Figueredo and João Paulo Cabral - Facilitating with BTP at Cornélio Brennand Group
Frederico Gurgel - GFX Payment with Cards Integrated into S/4HANA Public Edition
Danilo C. Bispo - Demystifying SAP DRC NFe: How to Prepare for a Migration?
João Otto Gethmann Jr. and Alan Dias - Innovation in SAP

As is tradition, the event concluded with exciting prize giveaways. Among the items raffled were books from SAP Press, courses from Moovi Education, and electronic equipment from MakeValue, providing a thrilling finish to a day that will certainly be remembered by all attendees.

This marked the fourth edition of SAP Inside Track in Recife, reinforcing the growth of the SAP market in the region, with new partners emerging and skilled professionals ready to implement the latest technologies. We can hardly wait for the next edition, already in the works for 2024.

In the meantime, keep an eye on the upcoming Warm-up SITFOR, SIT Fortaleza, and SIT Curitiba events, which are in full swing! There’s still time to join this great gathering of the SAP community, filled with learning and networking opportunities. Be sure to follow the links below to stay updated on everything happening in the SAP world here in Brazil.

LinkedIn Pages:
SAP Inside Track Brazil

SAP Inside Track YouTube Channel

Fortaleza Group

SAP Inside Track Curitiba Event

YouTube Channels:
https://www.youtube.com/@SAPInsideTrack

https://www.youtube.com/@sitfor-sapinsidetrackforta6071

We thank everyone who participated and those who couldn't make it don't miss our upcoming events! Follow our group to stay in the loop with all the latest news. Big hugs to all!

entry.title: Active Metrics in Alerting Directory Browser not having any data in EC2 MAI Support Servlet
entry.content:
Hi,

We are receiving very limited metrics ( mostly availability) when we check E2E PERSON_NAME Support Servlet ( Show Collection Statistics).

When we checked using 'Active Metrics in Alerting Directory Browser' (MAI_TOOLS) those are active. Even Systems Monitoring showing data for it. 

But same data is not visible in E2E Support Servlet.

What should be done to get data for those metrics?

Also

Is there any way to get the metrics with PULL_RFC collector?

Can we use any out of the box way/function/transaction to get these metrics?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Active Metrics in Alerting Directory Browser not havi...
entry.content:
Dear Customer

Support Servlet will show only metrics from collectors of type PUSH_GENERIC for this reason not all metrics you see in Directory Browser will be shown.

It is not possible to change the data collector for all metrics to PUSH_GENERIC, as certain metrics are designed to work with specific data collectors. Changing the data collector would cause gray metrics.

The Support Servlet works only with PUSH_GENERIC metrics.

Please see: E2E MAI Support Servlet

See also: 2355191 - How to use "E2E MAI Support Servlet" in tx MAI_Tools for Technical Monitoring troubleshooting 

To to get the metrics with PULL_RFC collector please check the KBA: 2016768 - Data Provider Check for PULL_RFC Metrics using transaction MAI_Tools

entry.title: Re: Show more than one currency in the same planning view
entry.content:
Hi,

If a key figure has base planning level with currency dimension for e.g., WKPRODLOCCURR, add exchange rate conversion WKPRODLOCCURRCURRTO. Refer any standard key figure which has currency conversion modeled. Pull attribute currency to or currency to description in the planning view. Make sure exchange rates are maintained for both currencies. Please post if it works.

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Upgrading Oracle 1to 9c

Also, did you applied the most recent SAP bundle (ORACLE patches) before starting the DB upgrade?

entry.title: User status - business transaction RMWQ and RMWA
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a problem with the user status of the production order.

My customer requested that when we GR the production order (DLV status) with PP, QM module, we cannot withdraw any more components and confirm the operation.

I have set the user status. It works, blocking the step confirmation. However, it cannot block the material consumption. I have set the prohibited business transaction "Goods Movement", which means blocking the "GI for production order". But when set like that, the system blocks the Quantity posted transaction (mvt 321) when we have the UD code.

Please suggest how to implement this.

Regards,

Jack

entry.title: Is it possible to remove a material extension from a distribution chain?
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

We have a scenario where we've extended a material to a distribution chain (ie 1710 10).  However, we have since decided to use common distribution channels and divisions, and have assigned them in customizing as such.  So we've also extended this material to the common one (ie 1710 00), which we will be using going forward.  However, since the distribution chain 1710 10 is no longer valid, whenever I try to edit this product in the application Manage Product Master Data it keeps giving me an error saying that I've maintained an invalid sales area, and I'm unable to save any changes at all.  I've tried setting the deletion indicator at the distribution chain layer, and it's still hitting me with this error.  Can anything be done to remove this extension so it stops giving me a hard error?   

Thanks

entry.title: Is Single Tenancy for LLMs Available in SAP GenAI Hub or Planned for Future Releases?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,
 
I’m exploring options for integrating Large Language Models (LLMs) within our SAP landscape, specifically through the SAP GenAI Hub. One of our key requirements is ensuring single tenancy, where the LLMs are dedicated exclusively to our organization, and no infrastructure or data is shared with other clients.
 
I haven’t come across explicit details on whether single tenancy is a feature currently offered by the SAP GenAI Hub for LLMs, or if this is something SAP plans to support in future releases.
 
Can anyone clarify:
 
1. Is single tenancy for LLMs available on the SAP GenAI Hub?
2. If not, are there any plans in SAP’s roadmap to introduce this feature, particularly for customers with strict data privacy and isolation requirements?
3. Are there recommended alternative approaches to achieve single-tenancy or similar levels of isolation if this feature is not currently available?
 
Thanks in advance for your insights!

entry.title: Re: Custom Alert for different periods
entry.content:
Hi Mariia,

This is not possible. You can offset key figure values in past by a year (for e.g., Actuals Qty Prior Year) and then compare this key figure instead in the alert condition.

Regards,

Piyush

entry.title: Extract KNA1 General Customer Master Data in CSV File Using ABAP Program
entry.content:
ABAP PROGRAM(S) - CUSTOMER 

ABAP Program Name - Z_KNA1_CUSTOMER 

  • Program Type - ABAP 
  • ABAP Program Name - Z_KNA1_CUSTOMER 
  • TCODE – Z_KNA1_CUST
  • Output File Location - “C:\tmp” 
  • Output File Name- KNA1.CSV 
  • Output File Type – CSV (Column with Semicolons Separator (;) ) 
  • SAP Table Name – KNA1 & KNB1 
  • List Of Fields to Be Extracted - 
  • KUNNR 
  • MANDT 
  • LAND1 
  • MCOD1 
  • NAME1 
  • NAME2 
  • NAME3 
  • STRAS 
  • ORT01 
  • PSTLZ 
  • SPRAS 
  • LZONE 
  • KTOKD 

---------------Program Start From Below-----------------------------

REPORT Z_KNA1_CUSTOMER.
TYPE-POOLS: VRM.

DATA: NAME TYPE VRM_ID,
LIST TYPE VRM_VALUES,
VALUE LIKE LINE OF LIST.


PARAMETERS: ENTITY(10) AS LISTBOX VISIBLE LENGTH 10.
PARAMETERS : OPATH TYPE STRING DEFAULT '\\ServerName\tmp\KNA1.csv'.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.

NAME = 'ENTITY'.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK01'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK01'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK75'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK75'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK21'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK21'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK31'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK31'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK41'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK41'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK02'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK02'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK73'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK73'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK04'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK04'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK61'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK61'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

CALL FUNCTION 'VRM_SET_VALUES' EXPORTING ID = NAME VALUES = LIST.START-OF-SELECTION.

type-pools:TRUXS.
data: begin of itab occurs 0,
KUNNR like KNA1-KUNNR,
MANDT like KNA1-MANDT,
LAND1 like KNA1-LAND1,
MCOD1 like KNA1-MCOD1,
NAME1 like KNA1-NAME1,
NAME2 like KNA1-NAME2,
NAME3 like KNA1-NAME3,
STRAS like KNA1-STRAS,
ORT01 like KNA1-ORT01,
PSTLZ like KNA1-PSTLZ,
SPRAS like KNA1-SPRAS,
LZONE like KNA1-LZONE,
KTOKD like KNA1-KTOKD,
BUKRS like KNB1-BUKRS,
end of itab.

data: itab1 type TRUXS_T_TEXT_DATA.
SELECT KNA1~KUNNR
KNA1~MANDT
KNA1~LAND1
KNA1~MCOD1
KNA1~NAME1
KNA1~NAME2
KNA1~NAME3
KNA1~STRAS
KNA1~ORT01
KNA1~PSTLZ
KNA1~SPRAS
KNA1~LZONE
KNA1~KTOKD
KNB1~BUKRS
from KNA1 INNER JOIN KNB1 ON KNA1~KUNNR = KNB1~KUNNR
into table itab
WHERE KNB1~BUKRS = ENTITY.

CALL FUNCTION 'SAP_CONVERT_TO_CSV_FORMAT'
EXPORTING
I_FIELD_SEPERATOR = ','
TABLES
I_TAB_SAP_DATA = itab
CHANGING
I_TAB_CONVERTED_DATA = itab1
EXCEPTIONS
CONVERSION_FAILED = 1
OTHERS = 2
.
IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
MESSAGE ID SY-MSGID TYPE SY-MSGTY NUMBER SY-MSGNO
WITH SY-MSGV1 SY-MSGV2 SY-MSGV3 SY-MSGV4.
ENDIF.

CALL FUNCTION 'GUI_DOWNLOAD'
EXPORTING
filename = OPATH
TABLES
data_tab = itab1
EXCEPTIONS
OTHERS = 1.

entry.title: How to update custom workflow scenario id in standard Fiori apps manifest file?
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Currently, a standard Fiori app Credit Memo Request is having hardcoded scenario id in its manifest file in below path:

embeddedcomponents>workflowcomponents>settings>scenarioid

How to update the custom workflow scenarioid in standard apps manifest?

Current Approach,

I have extended app using Adaptation Project and created new section for Approval Details. I have used component usage : sap.io.bpm.sworkfloweditor.lib.reuse.stworkflowinstance. With this I am able to show table but its not showing any data. Also, I have checked in network tab, its not sending any requests.

entry.title: Re: Error Create Table Persistence and Generate Data
entry.content:
Hello Victor,

you must use ZTRAVEL_APP_VKT instead of ZTRAVEL_VKT after define table. Both the object name and the name in the source code must be the same.

entry.title: Re: Plant "phased" roll out of SAP S4/HANA for Product Co...
entry.content:
Hello

sorry to say.. this is not possible.... The activation of PC (Product Compliance) (and therefore move from EHS Classic to PC) can only be done by "process" (and not by plant, company code or whatever similar criteria)

C.B.

PS: Best knowlegde on my side: something like you would like to get is not on the roadmap of SAP and from my point of view will not come to the Roadmap of PC solution

PPS: honestly: this question you could ask SAP:

What i could imagine which might work (but please ask SAP): you have one S/4 HANA system: You have two clients. In Client X you use EHS Classic and in Client Y PC; but if this help... no idea

entry.title: Extract KNB1 Customer Master (Company CodE) Data in CSV File Using ABAP Program
entry.content:
ABAP Program - Z_KNB1_CUSTOMER

  • Program Type - ABAP 
  • ABAP Program Name - Z_KNB1_CUST 
  • Tcode - ZHM_KNB1_C  
  • Output File Location - “C:\tmp” 
  • Output File Name- KNB1.CSV 
  • Output File Type – CSV (Column with Semicolons Separator (;)) 
  • SAP Table Name - KNB1 
  • List Of Fields to Be Extracted -  
  • KUNNR 
  • ZWELS 
  • ZTERM 

--------ABAP CODE START FROM HERE-----------

REPORT Z_KNB1_CUSTOMER.


TYPE-POOLS: VRM.

DATA: NAME TYPE VRM_ID,
LIST TYPE VRM_VALUES,
VALUE LIKE LINE OF LIST.


PARAMETERS: ENTITY(10) AS LISTBOX VISIBLE LENGTH 10.
PARAMETERS : OPATH TYPE STRING DEFAULT '\\SERVERNAME\tmp\Uae\KNB1.csv'.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.

NAME = 'ENTITY'.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK01'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK01'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK75'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK75'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK21'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK21'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK31'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK31'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK41'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK41'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK02'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK02'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK73'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK73'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK04'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK04'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK61'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK61'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

CALL FUNCTION 'VRM_SET_VALUES' EXPORTING ID = NAME VALUES = LIST.

START-OF-SELECTION.

type-pools:TRUXS.
data: begin of itab occurs 0,
kunnr like knb1-kunnr,
zwels like knb1-zwels,
zterm like knb1-zterm,
BUKRS like knb1-BUKRS,
end of itab.
data: itab1 type TRUXS_T_TEXT_DATA.
select KNB1~kunnr
KNB1~zwels
KNB1~zterm
KNB1~BUKRS
FROM KNB1
into table itab
WHERE KNB1~BUKRS = ENTITY.

CALL FUNCTION 'SAP_CONVERT_TO_CSV_FORMAT'
EXPORTING
I_FIELD_SEPERATOR = ','
TABLES
I_TAB_SAP_DATA = itab
CHANGING
I_TAB_CONVERTED_DATA = itab1
EXCEPTIONS
CONVERSION_FAILED = 1
OTHERS = 2
.
IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
MESSAGE ID SY-MSGID TYPE SY-MSGTY NUMBER SY-MSGNO
WITH SY-MSGV1 SY-MSGV2 SY-MSGV3 SY-MSGV4.
ENDIF.

CALL FUNCTION 'GUI_DOWNLOAD'
EXPORTING
filename = OPATH
TABLES
data_tab = itab1
EXCEPTIONS
OTHERS = 1.

entry.title: Add action button on Custom Business Object exposed as service on BAS on which BAPI is called
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

Currently we are on S4HANA 2020 on premise. We have a requirement where we need to use Custom Business Object ( CBO ) which will be exposed as a service and config on BAS. 

We will be doing with all CRUD operation on the above application build from CBO and also had to trigger a BAPI call to create Sales Order on one action button. 

Please suggest how I can add the BAPI functionality here on Action button. Secondly, how we can add action button on UI build through CBO.

Best Regards

entry.title: Integrate external users to SF LMS
entry.content:
I wanted to see if anyone has integrated external users (non payroll who don’t feed over from SAP on an import file) such as agency temps, contractors to SF LMS.

We currently have them complete safety/compliance trainings through an external site but we would like to integrate them in SF LMS so they are auto-assigned trainings.

I know this can be done with IAS import and this will exclude them from SF platform but wanted to see if I can pick some brains on how others may be managing the process who use SAP HR to house all employees.

Lastly, if external users are in LMS, do managers see their assigned trainings or show up in LMS reporting for learning needs?

Thanks.

entry.title: Premium Hub CoE – DSC Knowledge Bits – Advanced Shipping & Receiving - Immediate Loading
entry.content:
In this blog post, I will explain how to configure Immediate Loading functionality in Advanced Shipping & Receiving, in addition to demonstrating the process steps for testing the happy flow scenario without freight unit or delivery split in TM and EWM.

For Scenario 2 - Partial loading with freight unit / delivery split, please check the follow-up blog post - Immediate Loading with Delivery Split.

With Immediate Loading functionality in ASR (SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02), loading of single handling units is now allowed, even if not all items/HUs of an Outbound Delivery Order are fully picked, packed or staged, which improves the following:

  • Decrease total lead time
  • Avoids overcrowded goods issue zones
  • Allows a more flexible start of loading


Background Information:
In a warehouse driven scenario, after picking and packing of some products into handling units, loading can start even if not all products which are part of Outbound Delivery Orders assigned to same Freight Order are picked and packed compeltely. This would contribute in optimizing overall warehouse execution time by allowing the warehouse operators to load HUs once staged without waiting for complete list of HUs to be staged.

Prerequisites:

NOTE: Advanced Shipping and Receiving should be configured for TM-EWM integration, for detailed steps on how to configure ASR scenario, please check SAP Note 3512117 - Advanced Shipping and Receiving - Cookbook.

Configuration Steps:

1- Create new Packaging Material Type (optional)
SPRO->Extended Warehouse Management->Cross-Process Settings->Handling Units->Basics->Define Packaging Material Types for Warehousing.


Figure 01 - Packaging Material Type

2- Assign Number Range to new Packaging Material Type (optional)
SPRO->Extended Warehouse Management->Cross-Process Settings->Handling Units->Basics->External Identification->Assign Number Range Intervals to Packaging Material Types.


Figure 02 - Assign NR to Packaging Material Type

3- Define new Packaging Material Type in ERP (optional)
SPRO->Logistics General->Handling Unit Management->Basics->Technical Basics->Define Packaging Material Types for Shipping.


Figure 03 - Define ERP Packaging Material Type

4- Specify how ''Packed for Shipping'' status should be updated for Packaging Material Type
SPRO->Extended Warehouse Management->Cross-Process Settings->Handling Units->Basics->Set HU to "Packed for Shipping".


Figure 04 - Define how Packing for Shipping status is updated

NOTE: ''Packed for Shipping - SPSH'' is a new HU status on header level for Immediate Loading scenario. You can configure this status to be updated either manually or automatically by setting one of the following options:  
- Manually: In this case, you set the status manually in Work Center: Packing - General or in the inbound process Package-Based Shipping from Production.
- At Picking: The status is set when the handling unit is assigned to an outbound delivery after the picking warehouse task has been confirmed.
- After Packing: The status is set when packing is complete in process-oriented storage control.
- At Staging: The status is set when the handling unit arrives at a staging area.

This status can also be set automatically if some other conditions are fulfilled, for more information please check: Packed for Shipping | SAP Help Portal

5- Set Up Access Sequence for Immediate Loading
Easy Access->Extended Warehouse Management->Settings->Advanced Shipping and Receiving->Set Up Access Sequence for Immediate Loading.


Figure 05 - Define ASR Immediate Loading relevance

6- Define ASR Immediate Loading relevance
Easy Access->Extended Warehouse Management->Settings->Advanced Shipping and Receiving->Configure Immediate Loading.


Figure 06 - Define ASR Immediate Loading relevance

7- Assign new Pacakging Material to a Packaging Material
Easy Access->Change Material (MM02)


Figure 07 - Create new Packaing Material

Testing Scenario and Steps:

NOTE: The following testing scenario has been executed on SAP S/4HANA OP2023 FPS02 with embedded EWM and TM solutions.

Scenario #1: Happy Flow scenario without Delivery Split:
In this scenario we will pick entire product quantity planned in outbound delivery order, then we do packing and setting of ''Packed for Shipping'' status manually for one HU out of 2 HUs and then we start loading of one HU without completing packing or setting the status for other planned HU in the outbound delivery order.

1.1- Create Outbound Delivery in Logistics Execution (Tx. VL01NO)


Figure 08 - Create outbound delivery without reference in LE

1.2- Create Picking Warehouse Tasks (Tx. /SCWM/PRDO)


Figure 09 - Create Picking WTs

1.3- Confirm Picking Warehouse Tasks (Tx. /SCWM/MON)


Figure 10 - Confirm Picking WTs

1.4- Pack Products into Shipping Handling Units  (Tx. /SCWM/PACK)


Figure 11 - Pack Products into SHUs

1.5- Set 'Packed for Shipping' status for one HU (Tx. /SCWM/MON)


Figure 12 - Set 'Packed for Shipping' status

Handling Units information will be communicated to Freight Order after setting Packed for Shipping status on HU in EWM.

1.6- Create Freight Order  (App. Create Road Freight Order)
1. Assign Outbound Delivery to Freight Order: Iterms -> Insert->Insert FUs Based on Base Document ID
2. Assign Carrier: Business Partner->Add Carrier 
3. Update Transportation Status: Execution Status->Set Ready for Transportation Status


Figure 13 - Create Freight Order

1.7- Loading first Handling Unit (App. Load or Unload Freight Order)
Execution Status->Set Items to Loaded


Figure 14 - Loading one HU

With the new HU header status (packing for shipping) now it's possible to start loading as soon as one HU is ready, although rest of HUs are not packed/ready yet.

1.8- Pack and Set Shipping Status for second HU (Tx. /SCWM/PACK)


Figure 15 - Packing second HU

1.9- Loading second HU (Tx. /SCWM/RFUI)
Outbound Proceses->Loading->Loading by HU


Figure 16 - Loading second HU

1.10- Set 'Ready for Shipping' Status on ODO Level (Tx. /SCWM/PRDO)


Figure 17 - Set to Ready for Shipping on ODO level

1.11- Check LE Outbound Delivery (Tx. VL03N)


Figure 18 - Check LE Outbound Delivery after update

Handling Units information will be communicated to LE outbound delivery after setting Ready for Shipping status on ODO level in EWM.

1.12- Post Goods Issue in EWM (Tx. /SCWM/PRDO)


Figure 19 - Post Goods Issue in EWM

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Conclusion:

Immediate Loading is a new feature of Advanced Shipping and Receiving that further optimises the outbound process and gives warehouses more flexibility during performing loading activities, especially in a delivery split scenario that automatically triggers the split of the freight unit and outbound delivery order in EWM and TM respectively.

In this blog post, I have tried to execute the process steps using a mix of different GUI transactions and Fiori applications to show the different options available during executing each step. Also, this blog focused on a warehouse driven scenario. However, immediate loading functionality is also supported in a transport-driven scenario.

For Scenario 2 - Partial Loading with freight unit / delivery split, please check the follow-up blog post - Immediate Loading with Delivery Split.

For more information about Immediate Loading feature, please check Immediate Loading | SAP Help Portal.

For more information about other new EWM features in S/4HANA 2023 FPS02, please check the following blog post by Florian Kuchta: SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Extended Warehouse Management Private Edition 2023 FPS02– What’s New.
 
Learn more about SAP Premium Engagement and how a global team of experts can help you to get the most out of your SAP solutions.

If you already are a Premium Engagement customer, contact your Technical Quality Manager (TQM) to discuss how Premium Engagement services can help you with a custom-tailored and efficient service portfolio to make your project a success!

#CoE EMEA SCM1

1.2- Create Picking Warehouse Tasks (Tx. /SCWM/PRDO)

1.4- Pack Products into Shipping Handling Units  (Tx. /SCWM/PACK)


Figure 11 - Pack Products into SHUs

Figure 12 - Set 'Packed for Shipping' status

3. Update Transportation Status: Execution Status->Set Ready for Transportation Status

entry.title: Re: Sales BOM
entry.content:
Hi Manish,

Please look through the scope item 31Q. This lists other options other than ERLA and LUMF. It is mentioned under https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/5d66b65334e6b54ce10000000a174cb4.html as well. 

CPFH has pricing at header but the inventory is at item level.

It SSCUI (102807) is the "Assign Item categories" configuration node. However, we have locked it to provide assignment for few item category group and sales order types. I would still recommend looking through standard options in 31Q.

Regards,

Dennis
